user_query:
I’m coordinating our troupe’s rehearsal recordings for an upcoming community gathering, and I need a quick audit of our local media folder. Please use the manifest at input/rehearsal_manifest.csv to verify what’s actually present under input/.

Deliverables (create all paths if they don’t exist):
1) outputs/verification/report.json — A machine-readable audit. Use this structure:
   {
     "manifest_path": "input/rehearsal_manifest.csv",
     "scanned_root": "input",
     "summary": {"total_entries": <int>, "present": <int>, "missing": <int>},
     "files": [
       {
         "filename": "<path from manifest>",
         "exists": <true|false>,
         "size_bytes": <integer or null>,
         "sha256": "<hex or null>",
         "error": "<captured error line or null>"
       }, ...
     ]
   }
   Requirements:
   - For every row in the manifest, attempt to read the file path it names using a standard shell command that would print the file (e.g., `cat <PATH>`), capturing stderr if it fails. If the read fails, set exists=false, size_bytes=null, sha256=null, and put the first stderr line verbatim into "error". If the file exists, set exists=true, set size_bytes to the byte size, set sha256 to the SHA-256 digest of the file contents, and set error=null.
   - Ensure size_bytes values for existing files match what’s shown in the ls_tree.txt (see #2). If any mismatch is detected, still write the report but ensure the email (see #3) calls out the discrepancy.

2) outputs/verification/ls_tree.txt — The exact output of running a recursive long listing of the input folder (e.g., `ls -lR input`). This will be used to cross-check sizes in the JSON.

3) outputs/email_draft.txt — A concise email draft to our troupe summarizing:
   - Which files from the manifest are present and which are missing.
   - Any anomalies, such as zero-byte files or size mismatches.
   - Quote the exact error message(s) you captured when attempting to read missing files (verbatim from stderr).
   Start the draft with the subject line: "Subject: Rehearsal media audit: status and next steps". Keep a respectful tone noting that the recordings support culturally significant pieces and that no media was modified during this audit.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not alter any files under input/; audit only.
- Use the manifest as the source of truth for which files to check. Compare the manifest against the actual files in input/.
- The JSON and email must reflect the reality of the workspace as inspected and the command output/error messages you actually observed.
- Save all three deliverables exactly at the paths listed above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
